title: logformatter: include base SHA, with history link
body:
This is to help me in my flake analysis.

Scenario: I'm looking at recent flakes, and see Flake X. I know
that Flake X was "fixed" two weeks ago. Did the fix not work?
Or is this just one of those PRs that was created three weeks
ago (or three months ago) and never rebased to pick up the fix?
This adds a one-click way for me to tell.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Would've loved to ci-colon-docs this to save cycles, but I need to confirm that it works.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

/hold

I think I want to add a `Date` header; and I'd like to take some time to see what else could be helpful.

Never mind. There's no robust way to get the job-run time, only the time of processing the log. Which is available at bottom (signature) anyway. I think this is ready to go.

/hold cancel
